title: Index Multiple Selection
summary: Feature parent for the shipped checkbox foundation and separately approved selection consumers.
content_html:
Index Multiple Selection

Purpose

Add explicit checkbox selection to the manage-mode index, then let deliberately designed batch actions consume that selection without changing the displayed document.

This is the feature parent. It does not act as an implementation tracker.

Feature Documents

  • Concept — desired interaction, product boundaries, and deferred choices.
  • Architecture — proposed state, ownership, action-target, drag/drop, and service boundaries.
  • Delivery — shipped checkbox-selection foundation, reload behavior, and acceptance evidence.
  • Consumer Assessment — candidate classification, provisional value-to-risk order, exclusions, and delivery gate.
  • Docs Viewer Roadmap — scheduling, dependencies, and suggested feature order.

Current State

The checkbox-selection foundation is complete. The manage tree exposes Select, a sibling checkbox gutter, Shift-range selection, a selected count, Clear, and Done. docs-viewer-index-selection.js independently owns checked ids, the range anchor, mode, and reload/lifecycle reconciliation. Existing actions still resolve their prior targets except Prepare package, whose Action and compact package workflow now use checked ids as their only document target. Runtime Module Ownership records the shipped foundation.

Prepare package is the first shipped consumer. Its completed delivery covers Action registration, selection-only target resolution, the compact workflow, dedicated-route retirement, and focused verification; Prepare Package is the user workflow. Export, Move, and Delete still require separate delivery decisions, so this feature parent remains in progress. Ordinary Import is not a selection consumer, and Returned packages remains a whole-package scope workflow with no index-selection input.
